name: GSC MIxS human gut
description: Genomic Standards Consortium package extension for reporting of measurements and observations obtained from the environment where the sample was obtained. By choosing the environmental package, a selection of fields can be made from a relevant subsets of the GSC terms.

fields.label: project name
fields.name: project_name
fields.helpText: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

fields.label: experimental factor
fields.name: experimental_factor
fields.helpText: Experimental factors are essentially the variable aspects of an experiment design which can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI). For a browser of EFO (v 2.95) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/EFO; for a browser of OBI (v 2018-02-12) terms please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/OBI. E.g. time series design [EFO:EFO_0001779]

fields.label: ploidy
fields.name: ploidy
fields.helpText: The ploidy level of the genome (e.g. allopolyploid, haploid, diploid, triploid, tetraploid). It has implications for the downstream study of duplicated gene and regions of the genomes (and perhaps for difficulties in assembly). For terms, please select terms listed under class ploidy (PATO:001374) of Phenotypic Quality Ontology (PATO), and for a browser of PATO (v 2018-03-27) please refer to http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/PATO

fields.label: number of replicons
fields.name: number_of_replicons
fields.helpText: Reports the number of replicons in a nuclear genome of eukaryotes, in the genome of a bacterium or archaea or the number of segments in a segmented virus. Always applied to the haploid chromosome count of a eukaryote. Mandatory for MIGS of eukaryotes, bacteria, archaea and segmented virus.

fields.label: extrachromosomal elements
fields.name: extrachromosomal_elements
fields.helpText: Do plasmids exist of significant phenotypic consequence (e.g. ones that determine virulence or antibiotic resistance). Megaplasmids? Other plasmids (borrelia has 15+ plasmids).

fields.label: estimated size
fields.name: estimated_size
fields.helpText: The estimated size of the genome (in bp) prior to sequencing. Of particular importance in the sequencing of (eukaryotic) genome which could remain in draft form for a long or unspecified period. Mandatory for MIGS of eukaryotes.

fields.label: reference for biomaterial
fields.name: reference_for_biomaterial
fields.helpText: Primary publication if isolated before genome publication; otherwise, primary genome report. Mandatory for MIGS of bacteria and archaea.

fields.label: annotation source
fields.name: annotation_source
fields.helpText: For cases where annotation was provided by a community jamboree or model organism database rather than by a specific submitter

fields.label: sample volume or weight for DNA extraction
fields.name: sample_volume_or_weight_for_dna_extraction
fields.helpText: Volume (ml) or mass (g) of total collected sample processed for DNA extraction. Note: total sample collected should be entered under the term 'sample size'.

fields.label: nucleic acid extraction
fields.name: nucleic_acid_extraction
fields.helpText: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

fields.label: nucleic acid amplification
fields.name: nucleic_acid_amplification
fields.helpText: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the enzymatic amplification (PCR, TMA, NASBA) of specific nucleic acids

fields.label: library size
fields.name: library_size
fields.helpText: Total number of clones in the library prepared for the project

fields.label: library reads sequenced
fields.name: library_reads_sequenced
fields.helpText: Total number of clones sequenced from the library

fields.label: library construction method
fields.name: library_construction_method
fields.helpText: Library construction method used for clone libraries

fields.label: library vector
fields.name: library_vector
fields.helpText: Cloning vector type(s) used in construction of libraries

fields.label: library screening strategy
fields.name: library_screening_strategy
fields.helpText: Specific enrichment or screening methods applied before and/or after creating clone libraries in order to select a specific group of sequences

fields.label: target gene
fields.name: target_gene
fields.helpText: Targeted gene or locus name for marker gene studies

fields.label: target subfragment
fields.name: target_subfragment
fields.helpText: Name of subfragment of a gene or locus. Important to e.g. identify special regions on marker genes like V6 on 16S rRNA

fields.label: pcr primers
fields.name: pcr_primers
fields.helpText: PCR primers that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. This field should contain all the primers used for a single PCR reaction if multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction. The primer sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

fields.label: multiplex identifiers
fields.name: multiplex_identifiers
fields.helpText: Molecular barcodes, called Multiplex Identifiers (MIDs), that are used to specifically tag unique samples in a sequencing run. Sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

fields.label: adapters
fields.name: adapters
fields.helpText: Adapters provide priming sequences for both amplification and sequencing of the sample-library fragments. Both adapters should be reported; in uppercase letters

fields.label: pcr conditions
fields.name: pcr_conditions
fields.helpText: Description of reaction conditions and components for PCR in the form of 'initial denaturation:94degC_1.5min; annealing=...'

fields.label: sequencing method
fields.name: sequencing_method
fields.helpText: Sequencing machine used. Where possible the term should be taken from the OBI list of DNA sequencers (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0400103).

fields.label: sequence quality check
fields.name: sequence_quality_check
fields.helpText: Indicate if the sequence has been called by automatic systems (none) or undergone a manual editing procedure (e.g. by inspecting the raw data or chromatograms). Applied only for sequences that are not submitted to SRA or DRA

fields.label: chimera check software
fields.name: chimera_check_software
fields.helpText: Tool(s) used for chimera checking, including version number and parameters, to discover and remove chimeric sequences. A chimeric sequence is comprised of two or more phylogenetically distinct parent sequences.

fields.label: relevant electronic resources
fields.name: relevant_electronic_resources
fields.helpText: A related resource that is referenced, cited, or otherwise associated to the sequence in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

fields.label: relevant standard operating procedures
fields.name: relevant_standard_operating_procedures
fields.helpText: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

fields.label: negative control type
fields.name: negative_control_type
fields.helpText: The substance or equipment used as a negative control in an investigation

fields.label: positive control type
fields.name: positive_control_type
fields.helpText: The substance, mixture, product, or apparatus used to verify that a process which is part of an investigation delivers a true positive.

fields.label: collection date
fields.name: collection_date
fields.helpText: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

simpleValidation.pattern: (^[12][0-9]{3}(-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?(/[0-9]{4}(-[0-9]{2}(-[0-9]{2}(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?)?$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)
simpleValidation.patternMessage: Must match pattern: (^[12][0-9]{3}(-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?(/[0-9]{4}(-[0-9]{2}(-[0-9]{2}(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?)?$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)

fields.label: geographic location (country and/or sea)
fields.name: geographic_location_country_and_or_sea
fields.helpText: The location the sample was collected from with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).

options.value: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
options.label: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

fields.label: geographic location (latitude)
fields.name: geographic_location_latitude
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

simpleValidation.pattern: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)
simpleValidation.patternMessage: Must match pattern: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)

fields.label: geographic location (longitude)
fields.name: geographic_location_longitude
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

simpleValidation.pattern: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)
simpleValidation.patternMessage: Must match pattern: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)

fields.label: geographic location (region and locality)
fields.name: geographic_location_region_and_locality
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.

fields.label: broad-scale environmental context
fields.name: broad_scale_environmental_context
fields.helpText: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

fields.label: local environmental context
fields.name: local_environmental_context
fields.helpText: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

fields.label: environmental medium
fields.name: environmental_medium
fields.helpText: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).

fields.label: source material identifiers
fields.name: source_material_identifiers
fields.helpText: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2).

fields.label: sample material processing
fields.name: sample_material_processing
fields.helpText: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed.

fields.label: isolation and growth condition
fields.name: isolation_and_growth_condition
fields.helpText: Publication reference in the form of pubmed ID (pmid), digital object identifier (doi) or url for isolation and growth condition specifications of the organism/material. Mandatory for MIGS and MIMARKS Specimen.

fields.label: propagation
fields.name: propagation
fields.helpText: The type of reproduction from the parent stock. Values for this field is specific to different taxa. For phage or virus: lytic/lysogenic/temperate/obligately lytic. For plasmids: incompatibility group. For eukaryotes: sexual/asexual. Mandatory for MIGs of eukayotes, plasmids and viruses.

fields.label: amount or size of sample collected
fields.name: amount_or_size_of_sample_collected
fields.helpText: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected.

fields.label: host body product
fields.name: host_body_product
fields.helpText: substance produced by the body, e.g. stool, mucus, where the sample was obtained from. For Foundational Model of Anatomy Ontology (FMA) (v 3.1) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/FMA

fields.label: medical history performed
fields.name: medical_history_performed
fields.helpText: whether full medical history was collected

fields.label: oxygenation status of sample
fields.name: oxygenation_status_of_sample
fields.helpText: oxygenation status of sample

fields.label: organism count
fields.name: organism_count
fields.helpText: Total cell count of any organism (or group of organisms) per gram, volume or area of sample, should include name of organism followed by count. The method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) Should also be provided. (example: total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qpcr)

fields.label: sample storage duration
fields.name: sample_storage_duration
fields.helpText: duration for which sample was stored

fields.label: sample storage temperature
fields.name: sample_storage_temperature
fields.helpText: temperature at which sample was stored, e.g. -80

fields.label: sample storage location
fields.name: sample_storage_location
fields.helpText: location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room

fields.label: sample collection device
fields.name: sample_collection_device
fields.helpText: The device used to collect an environmental sample. It is recommended to use terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO) and/or terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094).

fields.label: sample collection method
fields.name: sample_collection_method
fields.helpText: The method employed for collecting the sample. Can be provided in the form of a PMID, DOI, url or text.

fields.label: gastrointestinal tract disorder
fields.name: gastrointestinal_tract_disorder
fields.helpText: History of gastrointestinal tract disorders; can include multiple disorders. History of blood disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, gastrointestinal system disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:77).

fields.label: liver disorder
fields.name: liver_disorder
fields.helpText: History of liver disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, liver disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:409).

fields.label: host disease status
fields.name: host_disease_status
fields.helpText: list of diseases with which the host has been diagnosed; can include multiple diagnoses. The value of the field depends on host; for humans the terms should be chosen from DO (Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, other hosts are free text

fields.label: host subject id
fields.name: host_subject_id
fields.helpText: a unique identifier by which each subject can be referred to, de-identified, e.g. #131

fields.label: IHMC medication code
fields.name: ihmc_medication_code
fields.helpText: can include multiple medication codes

fields.label: host age
fields.name: host_age
fields.helpText: age of host at the time of sampling; relevant scale depends on species and study, e.g. could be seconds for amoebae or centuries for trees

fields.label: host body site
fields.name: host_body_site
fields.helpText: name of body site where the sample was obtained from, such as a specific organ or tissue (tongue, lung etc...). For Foundational Model of Anatomy Ontology (FMA) (v 3.1) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/FMA

fields.label: host height
fields.name: host_height
fields.helpText: the height of subject

fields.label: host body-mass index
fields.name: host_body_mass_index
fields.helpText: body mass index, calculated as weight/(height)squared

fields.label: ethnicity
fields.name: ethnicity
fields.helpText: A category of people who identify with each other, usually on the basis of presumed similarities such as a common language, ancestry, history, society, culture, nation or social treatment within their residing area. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_contemporary_ethnic_groups

fields.label: host occupation
fields.name: host_occupation
fields.helpText: most frequent job performed by subject

fields.label: host total mass
fields.name: host_total_mass
fields.helpText: total mass of the host at collection, the unit depends on host

fields.label: host phenotype
fields.name: host_phenotype
fields.helpText: phenotype of host. For Phenotypic quality Ontology (PATO) (v 2013-10-28) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/PATO

fields.label: host body temperature
fields.name: host_body_temperature
fields.helpText: core body temperature of the host when sample was collected

fields.label: host sex
fields.name: host_sex
fields.helpText: Gender or sex of the host.

fields.label: temperature
fields.name: temperature
fields.helpText: temperature of the sample at time of sampling

fields.label: salinity
fields.name: salinity
fields.helpText: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater.

fields.label: special diet
fields.name: special_diet
fields.helpText: specification of special diet; can include multiple special diets

fields.label: host diet
fields.name: host_diet
fields.helpText: type of diet depending on the host, for animals omnivore, herbivore etc., for humans high-fat, Mediterranean etc.; can include multiple diet types

fields.label: host last meal
fields.name: host_last_meal
fields.helpText: content of last meal and time since feeding; can include multiple values

fields.label: host family relationship
fields.name: host_family_relationship
fields.helpText: relationships to other hosts in the same study; can include multiple relationships

fields.label: host genotype
fields.name: host_genotype
fields.helpText: observed genotype

fields.label: host pulse
fields.name: host_pulse
fields.helpText: resting pulse, measured as beats per minute

fields.label: subspecific genetic lineage
fields.name: subspecific_genetic_lineage
fields.helpText: Information about the genetic distinctness of the sequenced organism below the subspecies level, e.g., serovar, serotype, biotype, ecotype, or any relevant genetic typing schemes like Group I plasmid. Subspecies should not be recorded in this term, but in the NCBI taxonomy. Supply both the lineage name and the lineage rank separated by a colon, e.g., biovar:abc123.

fields.label: trophic level
fields.name: trophic_level
fields.helpText: Trophic levels are the feeding position in a food chain. Microbes can be a range of producers (e.g. chemolithotroph)

fields.label: relationship to oxygen
fields.name: relationship_to_oxygen
fields.helpText: Is this organism an aerobe, anaerobe? Please note that aerobic and anaerobic are valid descriptors for microbial environments

fields.label: known pathogenicity
fields.name: known_pathogenicity
fields.helpText: To what is the entity pathogenic, for instance plant, fungi, bacteria

fields.label: encoded traits
fields.name: encoded_traits
fields.helpText: Should include key traits like antibiotic resistance or xenobiotic degradation phenotypes for plasmids, converting genes for phage

fields.label: observed biotic relationship
fields.name: observed_biotic_relationship
fields.helpText: Is it free-living or in a host and if the latter what type of relationship is observed

fields.label: chemical administration
fields.name: chemical_administration
fields.helpText: list of chemical compounds administered to the host or site where sampling occurred, and when (e.g. antibiotics, N fertilizer, air filter); can include multiple compounds. For Chemical Entities of Biological Interest ontology (CHEBI) (v111), please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/CHEBI

fields.label: perturbation
fields.name: perturbation
fields.helpText: type of perturbation, e.g. chemical administration, physical disturbance, etc., coupled with time that perturbation occurred; can include multiple perturbation types
